import io
import string
import list

fn parse_ints(_ s: String) -> List<Int>
  mut result: List<Int> = []
  let n = string.len(s)
  mut i = 0
  mut cur = 0
  mut has = false
  mut neg = false
  while i < n
    let b = match string.byte_at(s, i)
    when Some(v)
      v
    when None
      0
    end
    if b == 32 or b == 9 or b == 13
      if has
        if neg
          result = list.push(result, 0 - cur)
        else
          result = list.push(result, cur)
        end
        has = false
        cur = 0
        neg = false
      end
    else if b == 45
      neg = true
    else
      cur = cur * 10 + (b - 48)
      has = true
    end
    i = i + 1
  end
  if has
    if neg
      result = list.push(result, 0 - cur)
    else
      result = list.push(result, cur)
    end
  end
  result
end

let line1 = match io.read_line()
when Some(s)
  s
when None
  ""
end

let line2 = match io.read_line()
when Some(s)
  s
when None
  ""
end

let nums = parse_ints(line1)
let trimmed = string.trim(line2)
let target_opt = string.parse_int(trimmed)

match target_opt
when Some(t)
  let n = list.len(nums)
  mut lo = 0
  mut hi = n - 1
  mut found = false
  mut idx = 0
  while lo <= hi and not found
    let mid = (lo + hi) / 2
    match list.get(nums, mid)
    when Some(v)
      if v == t
        found = true
        idx = mid
      else if v < t
        lo = mid + 1
      else
        hi = mid - 1
      end
    when None
      lo = hi + 1
    end
  end
  if found
    println("index=#{idx}")
  else
    println("not_found")
  end
when None
  println("not_found")
end
